items.title: SQL Injection Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify SQL injection by combining source-to-sink evidence with safe local payloads in an authorized sandbox.
verification_checks: Read the local code window and identify the source variable.
Trace whether the source reaches the SQL sink without parameter binding.
If a local target is running, send benign quote/boolean payloads and look for SQL error or response difference.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: Prepared statement with separate parameter binding is present.
The scanner matched a query template but not an executable sink.
The suspect value is a constant or strictly converted numeric value.
remediation: Use parameterized queries.
Avoid SQL string concatenation.

items.title: Command Injection Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify command injection only in local sandboxes by confirming attacker input controls command syntax or arguments.
verification_checks: Confirm source-to-sink reachability from untrusted input to command execution.
Check whether shell=True or shell string construction is used.
Prefer harness verification that mocks command execution instead of running destructive commands.
Only use harmless local payloads such as echo markers in authorized sandboxes.
false_positive_signals: The executable and arguments are fixed server-side values.
The command is called with an argument array and shell=False.
Input is selected from a strict allowlist.
remediation: Replace shell commands with library calls.
Use argument arrays and shell=False.

items.title: Path Traversal Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify path traversal by confirming attacker-controlled input reaches a filesystem API without canonicalization or a base-directory allowlist.
verification_checks: Confirm the user-controlled value is concatenated or joined into a filesystem path.
Check whether the code canonicalizes the path (realpath/abspath) and enforces an allowlisted base directory.
If a local target runs, send ../ or encoded ..%2f payloads for a known file (app config, /etc/passwd) and look for file contents in the response.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed instead of not_reproduced.
false_positive_signals: The resolved path is validated to stay under an allowlisted base directory.
The filename is a fixed server-side constant or comes from a strict allowlist.
The framework normalizes and rejects traversal sequences before use.
remediation: Canonicalize the path and confirm it stays under an allowlisted base directory.
Reject traversal sequences and use safe file APIs.

items.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify XSS by confirming untrusted input reaches an HTML/JS sink without contextual output encoding.
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input is written into an HTML, attribute, JS, or URL context.
Check whether the value is contextually encoded/escaped or passed through a sanitizer (e.g. DOMPurify, autoescape).
If a local target runs, submit a benign marker payload such as xss and confirm it renders unescaped.
Distinguish reflected vs stored vs DOM sinks; for DOM XSS trace the client-side sink.
false_positive_signals: Template auto-escaping is enabled and the value is not marked safe.
Output is passed through a vetted HTML sanitizer or strict encoder.
The value is placed in a non-executing context (e.g. text node with escaping).
remediation: Apply context-aware output encoding.
Keep template auto-escaping on and avoid safe/raw markers on untrusted data.

items.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify SSRF by confirming attacker-controlled input dictates the destination of a server-side outbound request without an allowlist.
verification_checks: Confirm the user-controlled value becomes the host/URL of an outbound request.
Check for an allowlist of schemes/hosts and blocking of internal ranges and cloud metadata endpoints.
In an authorized sandbox only, point the target at a controlled local collaborator or 169.254.169.254 and observe whether the server initiates the request.
Never probe third-party or production metadata endpoints.
false_positive_signals: The destination host/scheme is restricted by a strict allowlist.
The URL is a fixed server-side constant, not user-controlled.
Internal IP ranges and metadata IPs are explicitly blocked.
remediation: Enforce a scheme/host allowlist and block internal ranges and metadata IPs.
Resolve and validate the target host before requesting.

items.title: Insecure Deserialization Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify insecure deserialization by confirming untrusted data reaches an unsafe deserializer that can instantiate arbitrary objects, ideally via a function-level harness.
owasp: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input is passed to a deserialization sink.
Confirm the deserializer can instantiate arbitrary classes or invoke __reduce__/readObject gadgets.
Confirm no integrity check (HMAC/signature) protects the serialized blob.
Prefer a function-level harness that feeds a crafted-but-harmless payload rather than a full RCE chain.
false_positive_signals: Only json.loads or yaml.safe_load is used on the untrusted data.
The serialized blob is signed and verified before deserialization.
The input is a trusted, application-generated constant.
remediation: Prefer safe formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
If unavoidable, sign+verify payloads and restrict allowed classes.

items.title: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify SSTI by confirming untrusted input is compiled as part of a server-side template rather than passed as template data.
verification_checks: Confirm user input is concatenated into the template string itself, not just passed as a rendering variable.
Send a benign arithmetic marker such as {{7*191}} or ${7*191} and check whether it evaluates to 1337 in the response.
Identify the engine (Jinja2/Twig/Freemarker) to select the correct evaluation marker.
If no target exists, record dynamic_verdict=not_executed.
false_positive_signals: User input is passed only as a bound template variable, not compiled as template source.
The engine sandbox blocks attribute access and arithmetic evaluation.
Output is static and the marker is not evaluated.
remediation: Never build templates from user input; pass data as bound variables.
Use a sandboxed template environment where feasible.

items.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify XXE by confirming an XML parser processes untrusted input with external entity resolution enabled.
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted XML is parsed and external/parameter entities are not disabled.
In an authorized sandbox, submit a benign entity referencing a local file marker and check for reflection or an out-of-band callback.
Check parser configuration for resolve_entities/DTD/external-general-entities flags.
Prefer harmless local-file or collaborator markers over sensitive files.
false_positive_signals: The parser disables DTDs and external entity resolution.
A hardened/safe parser configuration (e.g. defusedxml) is used.
Input is not XML or is schema-validated without entity expansion.
remediation: Disable DTDs and external entity resolution (e.g. defusedxml).
Prefer non-XML formats or schema validation without entity expansion.

items.title: IDOR / Broken Access Control Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify IDOR by confirming an object is fetched by a user-supplied identifier without an ownership or authorization check.
verification_checks: Confirm the object is retrieved using a client-supplied identifier.
Confirm there is no ownership/role check tying the current session to the requested object.
In an authorized test, authenticate as user A and request an identifier owned by user B; a successful read/write confirms IDOR.
For mass assignment, confirm unexpected privileged fields (role/is_admin) are bindable from the request body.
false_positive_signals: An ownership or role check gates the object access.
Identifiers are unguessable capability tokens scoped to the session.
The framework enforces per-record authorization policies.
remediation: Enforce per-object ownership/authorization checks server-side.
Avoid exposing sequential IDs and bind only allowlisted fields.

items.title: Hardcoded Secret Verification Playbook
items.summary: Hardcoded secrets are a static finding with no runtime trigger; verification is manual triage of authenticity and impact rather than dynamic reproduction.
verification_checks: Confirm the literal is a real credential/key rather than a placeholder, example, or test fixture.
Determine whether the secret is reachable by runtime code and what system it authenticates to.
Check version-control history to see if the secret was ever pushed to a shared/remote repository.
Record dynamic_verdict=not_runtime_verifiable; this class is confirmed by manual review, not dynamic reproduction.
false_positive_signals: The value is an obvious placeholder (CHANGEME, xxxx, example).
The literal lives only in tests/fixtures and is not used in production.
The value is a public/non-secret identifier.
remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager.
Rotate any exposed secret and purge it from VCS history.

items.title: Code Injection Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify code injection by confirming user-controlled input reaches eval/exec-style execution rather than a safe parser.
verification_checks: Confirm attacker-controlled input reaches an eval/exec/code compilation sink.
Confirm the evaluated expression is not restricted to a fixed allowlist or parsed by a safe data parser.
Prefer a function-level harness that feeds a harmless marker expression and observes whether evaluation occurs.
false_positive_signals: ast.literal_eval or another data-only parser is used instead of eval/exec.
The expression is a fixed literal or selected from a strict allowlist.
The evaluator runs in a sandbox that blocks side effects and imports.
remediation: Remove eval/exec on untrusted input.
Use safe parsers or explicit allowlists for supported expressions.

items.title: NoSQL / LDAP / XPath Injection Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify non-SQL query injection by confirming untrusted input is concatenated into a query/filter expression without binding or escaping.
verification_checks: Identify the exact query language and whether user input changes query structure.
Confirm the value is concatenated into a filter/query string or object operator without binding.
Use benign syntax probes only against local authorized targets or a function harness.
false_positive_signals: The query builder binds values as data rather than query syntax.
Special characters/operators are escaped or rejected by a strict allowlist.
The candidate value is a fixed server-side constant.
remediation: Use parameterized query/filter APIs.
Escape query metacharacters and apply allowlists.

items.title: Arbitrary File Upload Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify arbitrary file upload by confirming attacker-controlled files can be stored in an executable or served location without strong validation.
verification_checks: Confirm uploaded filename, extension, or content type is attacker-controlled.
Confirm the file is stored in a served or executable location, or can be retrieved after upload.
In a local target, upload a harmless marker file and verify storage/retrieval behavior.
false_positive_signals: A strict extension/content allowlist blocks active content.
Files are renamed and stored outside the web root.
The upload endpoint scans or transforms files before storage.
remediation: Allowlist extensions and validate file content.
Store uploads outside web roots with random names.

items.title: Open Redirect / Header Injection Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify redirect and header injection issues by confirming user input controls redirect destinations or response header syntax.
verification_checks: Confirm the redirect target or header value is user-controlled.
Confirm destination validation is absent or only checks string prefixes.
For local targets, use a harmless external-looking URL or CRLF marker and inspect the Location/header response.
false_positive_signals: Only relative paths are allowed.
Destinations are validated against a normalized trusted-host allowlist.
Header values reject CR/LF characters before response construction.
remediation: Allowlist redirect destinations or require relative paths.
Reject CR/LF and normalize hosts before validation.

items.title: Weak Cryptography Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify weak cryptography by confirming weak algorithms or random generators protect security-sensitive data.
verification_checks: Confirm the algorithm/RNG is used for passwords, tokens, signatures, encryption, or session secrets.
Distinguish security use from non-security checksums, cache keys, or deduplication.
Record dynamic_verdict=not_runtime_verifiable unless a deterministic unit test can prove token predictability.
false_positive_signals: The weak hash is used only for non-security checksums or file deduplication.
A stronger algorithm is used on the actual security path.
The insecure RNG output is not used for secrets or authorization.
remediation: Use modern password hashing and authenticated encryption.
Use a CSPRNG for tokens and secrets.

items.title: Security Misconfiguration Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify insecure configuration by confirming the risky setting is active in the deployed or selected environment.
verification_checks: Confirm the insecure setting is active for production-like runtime, not only local development.
For headers/CORS, inspect an authorized local response and compare against expected policy.
For debug mode, check whether stack traces or debug consoles are reachable.
false_positive_signals: The setting is guarded by an environment check and disabled in production.
CORS is restricted to trusted origins despite permissive-looking defaults.
Security headers are applied by a proxy not visible in application code.
remediation: Disable debug in production.
Set strict security headers and CORS allowlists.

items.title: Vulnerable Dependency Verification Playbook
items.summary: Verify dependency findings by confirming the vulnerable package/version is installed and the affected feature is reachable.
verification_checks: Confirm the vulnerable version is present in the lockfile or resolved dependency tree.
Map the advisory to the package feature used by this project.
If exploitability depends on configuration or exposure, record those runtime conditions explicitly.
false_positive_signals: A patched version is actually installed despite a loose manifest range.
The advisory affects an unused feature or unreachable optional component.
The dependency is development-only and not shipped or deployed.
remediation: Upgrade to a patched version.
Remove unused vulnerable dependencies and add SCA checks.
